rationale_for_baseline_tier: An elderly patient living alone with recurrent postural presyncope and a near-collapse today needs an URGENT same-day in-person assessment (T3): the confirmatory orthostatic vitals and the ECG required to exclude an arrhythmic/ischaemic cause cannot be obtained via telehealth, and the fall risk is immediate. It is NOT T5/000 because the patient has recovered, is haemodynamically stable, and has no ongoing chest pain, breathlessness or loss of consciousness; it is NOT routine GP (T1/T2) because a cardiac cause is unexcluded and the patient could sustain an injurious fall before a routine appointment.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: True loss of consciousness, or a fall with a head strike or inability to get up
escalation_edges.rationale: Syncope with injury or a head strike in an anticoagulant-age patient is a time-critical emergency requiring ambulance assessment and transport.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you actually black out, hit your head, or fall and can't get up, call 000 for an ambulance straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Chest pain, palpitations or shortness of breath
escalation_edges.rationale: These may signal an acute cardiac (arrhythmic or ischaemic) cause and require emergency assessment rather than same-day review.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get chest pain, a racing or pounding heartbeat, or find it hard to breathe, call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Presyncope persisting or recurring despite withholding the drug and using counter-manoeuvres, or symptoms occurring while seated at rest
escalation_edges.rationale: Failure to improve on stopping the offending drug, or symptoms at rest, shifts suspicion toward a non-postural cause and warrants prompt in-person reassessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the dizziness keeps happening even after holding the tablet and moving slowly, or you feel it while just sitting, get seen in person the same day.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Recurrent near-falls in a patient who lives alone
escalation_edges.rationale: Living alone with recurrent orthostatic near-falls is itself a reason for urgent same-day assessment and interim fall-prevention, independent of the underlying cause.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because you live on your own and nearly fell, you should be seen in person today and keep support furniture close until then.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with no one present at home
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Raises the urgency of same-day in-person assessment and fall-prevention because an unwitnessed fall could result in a long-lie; consider whether a family member or neighbour can stay or check in.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Elderly (older adult) with a recent near-fall
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lowers the threshold for in-person cardiac exclusion and injury assessment; avoids over-sedating vestibular agents.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need to be seen in person today. Do not take any more of your prostate tablet until a doctor reviews it. Until you are seen, change position slowly — sit on the edge of the bed for two minutes, pump your legs, then stand — drink plenty of water, keep support furniture within reach, and remove any loose rugs. Call 000 immediately if you pass out, hit your head, cannot get up after a fall, or develop chest pain or breathlessness.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Arrange an urgent same-day in-person review (same-day GP, urgent care, or after-hours home-visit service); call 000 for the emergency triggers.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If it is after hours, use an after-hours home-visit doctor or an urgent-care service for the same-day assessment; 000 is available 24 hours for the emergency triggers.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Provide the reviewing clinician with the postural symptom pattern, the recent tamsulosin dose increase, the near-fall today, and the fact the patient lives alone, so orthostatic vitals, an ECG and a medication review can be done.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating this as routine/self-care and reassuring the patient at home without arranging same-day in-person orthostatic vitals and an ECG to exclude a cardiac cause.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An unexcluded intermittent arrhythmia later causes true syncope, or the patient sustains an injurious unwitnessed fall while living alone.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Deferring to a routine GP appointment over the coming days rather than a same-day assessment, risking a further near-fall or fall before the delayed appointment.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending a recovered, haemodynamically stable patient to the emergency department by ambulance when an urgent same-day in-person assessment would suffice — causing unnecessary ambulance/ED use and the iatrogenic risks of hospitalising an older adult.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the presyncope is purely orthostatic/iatrogenic or has a cardiac (arrhythmic/ischaemic) contribution that cannot be excluded via telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Name the postural pattern and the likely drug cause, withhold the offending drug, and arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment with orthostatic vitals and an ECG to exclude a cardiac cause, with clear cardiac/injury safety-netting.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Committing to a confident benign remote diagnosis and reassuring the patient into home-only management, or over-reacting with a 000 ambulance for a recovered stable patient.
